name: Audio

unions.name: Audio
unions.comment: Some kind of sound we want to play. To create `Audio` start with `audio`.

unions.name: LoadError
unions.comment: Possible errors we can get when loading audio files.

unions.name: Source
unions.comment: Audio data we can use to play sounds

aliases.name: LoopConfig
aliases.comment:
Control how audio loops. `loopEnd` defines where (relative to the start of the audio) the audio should loop and `loopStart` where it should loop to.

    -- Here we have a song that plays an intro once and then loops between the 10 second point and the end of the song.
    let
        default =
            Audio.audioDefaultConfig

        -- This package doesn't support getting how long a sound plays for so we need to hard code it instead.
        songLength =
            Duration.seconds 120
    in
    audioWithConfig
        { default | loop = Just { loopStart = Duration.seconds 10, loopEnd = songLength } }
        coolBackgroundMusic
        startTime

aliases.name: PlayAudioConfig
aliases.comment: Extra settings when playing audio from a file.
aliases.type: { loop : Maybe.Maybe Audio.LoopConfig, playbackRate : Basics.Float, startAt : Duration.Duration }

aliases.name: VolumeTimeline
aliases.comment:
A nonempty list of (time, volume) points for defining how loud a sound should be at any point in time.
The points don't need to be sorted but don't include multiple points that have the same time.

values.name: applicationWithAudio
values.comment: Browser.application but with the ability to play sounds.
values.type: { init : flags -> Url.Url -> Browser.Navigation.Key -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg, Audio.AudioCmd msg ), view : model -> Browser.Document msg, update : msg -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg, Audio.AudioCmd msg ), subscriptions : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, onUrlRequest : Browser.UrlRequest -> msg, onUrlChange : Url.Url -> msg, audio : model -> Audio.Audio, audioPort : Audio.Ports msg } -> Audio.Program flags model msg

values.name: audio
values.comment: Play audio from an audio source at a given time. This is the same as using `audioWithConfig audioDefaultConfig`.

values.name: audioWithConfig
values.comment: Play audio from an audio source at a given time with config.

values.name: cmdBatch
values.comment: Combine multiple commands into a single command. Conceptually the same as Cmd.batch.

values.name: cmdMap
values.comment: Map a command from one type to another. Conceptually the same as Cmd.map

values.name: cmdNone
values.comment: A command that does nothing. Conceptually the same as Cmd.none.

values.name: documentWithAudio
values.comment: Browser.document but with the ability to play sounds.
values.type: { init : flags -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg, Audio.AudioCmd msg ), view : model -> Browser.Document msg, update : msg -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg, Audio.AudioCmd msg ), subscriptions : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, audio : model -> Audio.Audio, audioPort : Audio.Ports msg } -> Audio.Program flags model msg

values.name: elementWithAudio
values.comment: Browser.element but with the ability to play sounds.
values.type: { init : flags -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg, Audio.AudioCmd msg ), view : model -> Html.Html msg, update : msg -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg, Audio.AudioCmd msg ), subscriptions : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, audio : model -> Audio.Audio, audioPort : Audio.Ports msg } -> Audio.Program flags model msg

values.name: lamderaFrontendWithAudio
values.comment: Lamdera.frontend but with the ability to play sounds (highly experimental, just ignore this for now).
values.type: { init : Url.Url -> Browser.Navigation.Key -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd frontendMsg, Audio.AudioCmd frontendMsg ), view : model -> Browser.Document frontendMsg, update : frontendMsg -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd frontendMsg, Audio.AudioCmd frontendMsg ), updateFromBackend : toFrontend -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd frontendMsg, Audio.AudioCmd frontendMsg ), subscriptions : model -> Platform.Sub.Sub frontendMsg, onUrlRequest : Browser.UrlRequest -> frontendMsg, onUrlChange : Url.Url -> frontendMsg, audio : model -> Audio.Audio, audioPort : Audio.Ports frontendMsg } -> { init : Url.Url -> Browser.Navigation.Key -> ( Audio.Model frontendMsg model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Audio.Msg frontendMsg) ), view : Audio.Model frontendMsg model -> Browser.Document (Audio.Msg frontendMsg), update : Audio.Msg frontendMsg -> Audio.Model frontendMsg model -> ( Audio.Model frontendMsg model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Audio.Msg frontendMsg) ), updateFromBackend : toFrontend -> Audio.Model frontendMsg model -> ( Audio.Model frontendMsg model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Audio.Msg frontendMsg) ), subscriptions : Audio.Model frontendMsg model -> Platform.Sub.Sub (Audio.Msg frontendMsg), onUrlRequest : Browser.UrlRequest -> Audio.Msg frontendMsg, onUrlChange : Url.Url -> Audio.Msg frontendMsg }

values.name: loadAudio
values.type: (Result.Result Audio.LoadError Audio.Source -> msg) -> String.String -> Audio.AudioCmd msg

values.name: mapUserMsg
values.comment: Change the `userMsg` type in `Model`. Useful for dealing with migrations in Lamdera.
values.type: (userMsgOld -> userMsgNew) -> Audio.Model userMsgOld userModel -> Audio.Model userMsgNew userModel

values.name: scaleVolume
values.comment: Scale how loud a given `Audio` is. If the the volume is less than 0, 0 will be used instead.

values.name: scaleVolumeAt
values.comment:
Scale how loud a given `Audio` is at given points in time. The volume will transition linearly between those points.

    import Audio
    import List.Nonempty exposing (Nonempty)
    import Time


    -- Here we define a function that fades in audio to full volume and then fades it out until it's muted.
    --
    --  1                 ________
    --                  /         \
    --  0 ____________/            \_______
    --     t ->   fade in        fade out
    fadeInOut fadeInTime fadeOutTime audio =
        Audio.scaleVolumeAt
            (Nonempty
                ( addSeconds -2 fadeInTime, 0 )
                [ ( fadeInTime, 1 )
                , ( fadeOutTime, 1 )
                , ( addSeconds 2 fadeOutTime, 0 )
                ]
            )
            audio

    addSeconds s =
        Time.posixToMillis >> ((+) s * 1000) >> Time.millisToPosix

values.name: silence
values.comment: The sound of no sound at all.

values.name: userModel
values.comment: Get the user state stored in `Model`.

values.name: withUserModel
values.comment: Set the user state stored in `Model`. Useful for dealing with migrations in Lamdera.
values.type: userModelNew -> Audio.Model userMsg userModelOld -> Audio.Model userMsg userModelNew
